title: Introduction
body: Welcome to Zyplo documentation.
Zyplo is a developer-focused project management tool designed to help teams plan faster and ship better.
This guide walks you through the core concepts, workflows, and productivity features.

title: Getting Started
body: Create an account and log in to Zyplo.
Create your first workspace for your team.
Create a project inside the workspace.
Invite teammates and assign roles.
Create and assign your first task.

subsections.title: Workspace
body: A workspace is your team’s main space in Zyplo.
It contains all your projects, members, and settings.
You can create multiple workspaces for different teams or companies.

subsections.title: Project
body: A project represents a product or codebase.
Each project lives inside a workspace.
Projects contain tasks, boards, and activity history.

subsections.title: Task
body: A task is a unit of work tracked in Zyplo.
Each task has a title, description, status, priority, assignee, and due date.
Tasks move across the board as work progresses.

subsections.title: The Board
body: The board shows tasks grouped by status.
Each column represents a workflow stage.
This gives a real-time view of project progress.

subsections.title: Columns
body: Columns represent steps like To Do, In Progress, Review, and Done.
You can customize columns per project.
Moving a card updates the task status.

subsections.title: Cards
body: Each task appears as a card.
Cards show key information like assignee and priority.
Click a card to open full task details.

title: Command Palette
body: Press Ctrl + K to open the command palette.
Jump to projects, create tasks, assign issues, and update status instantly.
This keeps you productive without leaving the keyboard.

title: Notifications & Activity
body: Get notified when tasks are assigned to you.
Track changes via the activity log.
Stay updated without checking every board manually.

title: Shortcuts & Tips
body: Ctrl + K opens the command palette.
Use labels to group related tasks.
Use priorities to plan sprints effectively.
Review your dashboard daily to stay aligned.
